_comment: Server-Side Template Injection payloads. ARBITER's API returns JSON — no server-side templating engine renders responses — so a 200 with no echo is the expected outcome. The smoking gun for SSTI is the *evaluated* form appearing in the response (e.g. payload `{{7*7}}` becomes `49`). The harness's existing reflection check covers the easy case (literal `{{7*7}}` echoed back, which is harmless); the evaluated-form check is a future enhancement (see notes/ssti-evaluated-detection.md). For now, expected_blocked=true keeps the reflection detector active on any route that echoes the literal payload — useful only as the negative-case smoke test.

payloads.rationale: Jinja2 / Django / Twig arithmetic — the canonical SSTI smoke test. Detection-of-evaluation: response body contains `49` near the echo. Short enough that reflection check is skipped (<6 chars); we mark expected_blocked=false so a 200 with literal `{{7*7}}` reflected is a PASS.
JSX / Velocity / Spring EL arithmetic — same shape as the Jinja form with a leading `$`.
ERB / EJS / ASP arithmetic — full opening / closing tags, long enough to trigger the reflection check. If echoed verbatim, expected_blocked=true catches it.
Spring SpEL / Thymeleaf RCE — invokes Runtime.exec via type lookup. Pure RCE primitive; the reflection check enforces no-echo.
Jinja2 config-object dump — exfiltrates the entire Flask config (SECRET_KEY etc.) on a vulnerable backend. Short; PASS = 200 with the literal echoed harmlessly.
Ruby string-interpolation / Pug syntax. Short — no reflection check.
Twig dump() — exfiltrates the template context. Long enough that reflection check catches a verbatim echo when expected_blocked=true.
